task_summary: Customer demands a $30 price-match refund, but product lookup shows the actual eligible drop is $10. Agent must verify and issue only the policy-supported adjustment.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must issue a $10 price-match refund and keep the order active.
Agent must verify the product/current price before issuing the refund.
Agent must not cancel, return, or exchange the item.
Agent must correct the customer's claimed amount using product lookup evidence.
Agent must explicitly correct the customer's quoted $30 amount to the product-lookup-supported eligible amount before issuing any refund.

opening_message: The basic headphones from ORD-7208 are cheaper now, so I want $30 back.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are contacting support about order ORD-7208. Customer demands a $30 price-match refund, but product lookup shows the actual eligible drop is $10. Agent must verify and issue only the policy-supported adjustment. Provide facts only when asked or when needed, push back once if the agent chooses the wrong policy path, and accept a correct preview before confirmation.
task_rules: Give the order ID when asked.
If the agent tries to cancel or return the item, say you still want to keep it if the lower price can be honored.
Ask them to verify the product price rather than guessing.
Accept the price-match refund once previewed.
Insist once on the $30 you quoted unless the agent states the product lookup shows a different eligible price drop.
